# cat.pol

Political and policy document classification with LLMs. A domain wrapper
around [cat.stack](https://christophersoria.com/cat-llm/cat.stack/) that
adds a registered-source fetcher (city ordinances, federal laws,
executive orders, presidential speeches, social-media archives) and
policy-document prompt framing.

`cat.pol` wraps the Python [catpol](https://pypi.org/project/cat-pol/)
package via [reticulate](https://rstudio.github.io/reticulate/).

## Installation

``` r

# From R-universe (recommended once published):
install.packages("cat.pol", repos = "https://chrissoria.r-universe.dev")

# Or from a local clone:
devtools::install("path/to/cat.stack")
devtools::install("path/to/cat.pol")

# Install the Python backend
# pip install cat-pol
```

## Quick Start

### Classify ordinances from a built-in source

``` r

library(cat.pol)

results <- classify(
  source     = "city_san_diego",
  doc_type   = "ordinance",
  since      = "2024-01-01",
  n          = 50,
  categories = c("Housing", "Public Safety", "Finance",
                 "Infrastructure", "Health"),
  api_key    = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY")
)
```

### Discover categories from your own text

``` r

result <- extract(
  input_data        = df$bill_text,
  document_context  = "California state legislation",
  api_key           = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY")
)
print(result$top_categories)
```

### Summarize policy documents in plain English

``` r

results <- summarize(
  source = "federal_executive_orders",
  since  = "2025-01-01",
  format = "paragraph",
  tone   = "eli5",
  api_key = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY")
)
```

### List every available data source

``` r

list_sources()
#> [1] "city_san_diego"            "city_san_francisco"
#> [3] "federal_laws"              "federal_executive_orders"
#> [5] "social_trump_truth"        ...
```

## Functions

| Function | Description |
|----|----|
| [`classify()`](https://christophersoria.com/cat-llm/cat.pol/reference/classify.md) | Classify policy documents into categories |
| [`extract()`](https://christophersoria.com/cat-llm/cat.pol/reference/extract.md) | Discover and extract categories from policy text |
| [`explore()`](https://christophersoria.com/cat-llm/cat.pol/reference/explore.md) | Get raw category extractions for saturation analysis |
| [`summarize()`](https://christophersoria.com/cat-llm/cat.pol/reference/summarize.md) | Summarize policy documents (with `tone` parameter) |
| [`list_sources()`](https://christophersoria.com/cat-llm/cat.pol/reference/list_sources.md) | List every registered political data source |

## License

MIT

# Package index

## Classify & extract policy documents

- [`classify()`](https://christophersoria.com/cat-llm/cat.pol/reference/classify.md)
  : Classify political and policy documents using LLMs
- [`extract()`](https://christophersoria.com/cat-llm/cat.pol/reference/extract.md)
  : Discover categories from political and policy documents using LLMs
- [`explore()`](https://christophersoria.com/cat-llm/cat.pol/reference/explore.md)
  : Explore raw categories in political and policy documents

## Summarize policy documents

- [`summarize()`](https://christophersoria.com/cat-llm/cat.pol/reference/summarize.md)
  : Summarize political and policy documents using LLMs

## Data sources

- [`list_sources()`](https://christophersoria.com/cat-llm/cat.pol/reference/list_sources.md)
  : List registered political data sources
